Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

I'm not sure this is correct for ldiskfs.  According to the ldev.conf.5 man page, the only allowed prefixes before the device-path are md: and zfs:.  Your new test seems to assume that ldiskfs: needs to be allowed, which may be new, undocumented functionality.

It was assumed in the past that a lack of zfs: label meant it was an ldiskfs filesystem.  I is not obvious to me that the default is currently coded in correctly.

If that has all been considered, at minimum this requires an update to ldev.conf.5.

Patch Set 6:

(4 comments)

Looks to me like this passes testing because (a) $num == "" when uninitialized, and (b) the fstype of the mds happens to be the same as that of the mgs.  If so, this should be fixed.

If I'm mistaken, let me know and I'll switch to +1.

parents.subject: LU-8055 ldev: File system label filtering

commit.subject: LU-8055 ldev: Add %H and %b to command sub
commit.message:
LU-8055 ldev: Add %H and %b to command sub

Introduced %H and %b substitutions for hostname
and backend file system, respectively.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=conf-sanity
Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Di Natale <dinatale2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: I9b50defee43307f614106de4ad75e1fa9267e23b
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20040
Reviewed-by: Olaf Faaland-LLNL <faaland1@llnl.gov>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Christopher J. Morrone <morrone2@llnl.gov>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
